title: Human exposure to microplastics: A review on exposure routes and public health impacts
source: Nawab et al., Journal of Hazardous Materials Advances, 2024

L2: Microplastic exposure occurs through ingestion, inhalation, and dermal contact pathways
Microplastics accumulate in human organs causing oxidative stress, inflammation, and endocrine disruption
L3: Comprehensive review of human exposure routes to microplastics including ingestion, inhalation, and skin contact, examining distribution and accumulation in organs, associated health impacts including oxidative stress, inflammation, endocrine disruption, and potential carcinogenesis
